title: ⚔️ After Action Report: The Dub Grumble Campaign

turns.agentText:
# ⚔️ After Action Report: The Dub Grumble Campaign

## WvW Squad Report — "Dub Grumble"
**June 13, 2026 · Commander: Dub Grumble · 40 vs. 49 avg.**

---

Tonight's squad ran **18 fights** at a consistent **1.2:1 numerical disadvantage**, finishing **9W / 9L**. Holding even against those odds is respectable, but a squad KDR of **0.70** (246 enemy deaths vs. 349 squad deaths) is the headline number to fix. Output wasn't the problem — survivability and discipline were.

| | |
|---|---|
| Fights | 18 (9W / 9L, 50%) |
| Avg. Squad vs. Enemy | 40 vs. 49 |
| Squad Deaths / Downs | 349 / 560 |
| Enemy Deaths / Downs | 246 / 437 |
| Squad KDR | **0.70** |

---

### Damage

Measured by what actually matters — **down contribution** — the Berserker pair of **BreakN.5496** and **DraGonfly.9317** led the raid, finishing nearly neck-and-neck at 1.08M and 1.05M respectively. **Mes.1906** (Reaper) earned his #3 spot through the combination of strip pressure and damage — a reminder that boon strip and down contribution aren't separate jobs for a well-played Reaper. **Fibbs.1623** rounds out the top four; his raw DPS led the squad, but translating that into downs at the level of the Berserkers is the next step.

{{figure}}

---

### Healing

The Druid core was a genuine bright spot. **Aeri.4529** topped both healing (6.7M) and cleanses (2,636), with **Arx.9785** close behind at 5.6M healed. Six Druids with staggered output — rather than one or two carrying it — gave the squad layered sustain through a long, punishing raid.

{{figure}}

---

### Support — Cleanses & Strips

Condition removal was well covered by the Druid lineup. Boon strip pressure came almost entirely from three Reapers — **Mes.1906** (1,223), **Jax Blake.7250** (968), and **General Moo.7930** (894) combining for 3,085 strips. That concentration is worth addressing: if any of them go down in a key fight, strip pressure collapses. Distributing that load more evenly across the Reaper core adds resilience.

{{figure}}

---

### ⚠️ Survivability & Areas of Improvement

Squad average was **~7.4 deaths per player**. The players below exceeded that in roles where it matters most:

| Player | Profession | Deaths | Downs |
|---|---|---|---|
| MonsterSully.6072 | Dragonhunter | 11 | 11 |
| hotep.8715 | Troubadour | 10 | 24 |
| bro.2856 | Troubadour | 9 | 16 |
| Kaliptuce.9051 | Firebrand | 9 | 22 |
| Subvert.7948 | Firebrand | 9 | 14 |

**hotep** and **Kaliptuce** are the biggest concern — high downs alongside high deaths means they're burning healer resources repeatedly without surviving. That's usually a positioning problem: too far outside the bubble or pre-stacking too early. **MonsterSully** logged 11 deaths in ~0.4 combat hours, the highest death rate on the squad.

More broadly, the enemy is finishing their downs more efficiently than we are. Tightening stomp and rally coordination across the board would move the KDR number without requiring any composition changes.

---

**Bottom line:** The healing roster is deep and the damage core is putting up the numbers that count. Closing the KDR gap next week comes down to support players staying alive longer and the squad converting downs more cleanly — get those right and the wins follow.
